data.sentence: Harold, can you hear us okay? I think Sofia just joined as well.
Yeah, I'm here. Good connection on my end.
Yep, I've got you both. Let me just close this other window... okay, yeah, I'm good.

Great. So Harold, thanks for making time today — I know you've got a lot going on with the quarter winding down. We wanted to get together to walk through the renewal terms and just, uh, make sure we're on the same page before anything needs to be signed.
Yeah, no, appreciate it. I've got about thirty, maybe thirty-five minutes so let's just get into it.

Sounds good. So I wanted to bring Sofia in because she can speak to any of the technical side of things if questions come up — especially around Detect and Comply, which I know are the two modules you've been using most heavily.
Hi Harold, good to meet you properly. I think we exchanged a couple of emails a few months back but this is the first time we've actually, uh, talked.

Right, right. Yeah I think that was around the onboarding stuff. Okay so — look, before we get into the contract specifics, I do want to bring up the outage from a couple weeks ago. That's kind of been sitting with me.
Yeah, absolutely, and I was going to bring that up myself. I didn't want to just skip over it. That was — that was not a good situation and I know it caused real disruption for your team.

Six hours, Daniel. Six hours where we had basically zero visibility into threat monitoring. In a manufacturing environment that's... I mean, our OT systems, our floor networks — we can't just go dark on threat detection for six hours.
Completely understood, and I don't want to minimize that at all. Harold, do you want me to walk through what we identified and what's been done since? I can get into the technical specifics.
Yeah, go ahead. I've seen the incident report but I want to hear it from someone who actually knows what they're talking about.

Sure. So the root cause was a single point of failure in our event ingestion pipeline — basically the primary processing node went down and there was no automatic failover in place at that layer. The cascading effect hit the whole detection feed. Since then we've deployed redundant processing nodes and implemented a circuit breaker pattern so that if one component fails, it isolates rather than cascades. We've been running that in production for about a week now and it's been stable.
Okay. And what's the — I guess, what's your confidence level that this doesn't happen again? Because that's really what I need to be able to tell my leadership.

I'd say we're significantly more resilient now than we were before. That specific failure mode is addressed. I won't tell you it'll never have any downtime ever, but a repeat of that exact scenario — the cascading failure — that's been architecturally corrected.

We're also — and this is something I can put in writing as part of the renewal package — we're offering an enhanced SLA on Detect uptime going forward. I want to make sure that's formalized, not just a verbal commitment.
That's good. I mean, that's the kind of thing I need to see on paper. What does that SLA look like, specifically?

We're proposing 99.9% uptime on Detect, which is up from the 99.5% that was in your current contract. And there are remediation credits tied to that if we fall below. I can send you the exact language after this call.
Alright. I'll have our legal team look at that language. Okay — so what else is on the table for renewal? What's the commercial side looking like?

So you're currently on a three-module package — Protect, Detect, and Comply. The base renewal would be a like-for-like at a roughly four percent uplift given, uh, standard CPI adjustments. We also have the option to add Identity if that's something you want to explore, but I don't want to push that today — I think the priority is getting the core renewal settled.

Yeah, Identity is not on the table right now. We're handling IAM internally and it's not a priority to change that in the near term. The four percent — look, I expected some kind of increase, but I'm going to need to justify that internally given what happened with the outage.

That's a fair point, and I'm not going to pretend the timing isn't awkward. What I'd like to do is see if there's a middle ground — maybe we can look at the structure of the deal, whether a two-year term instead of one year gives us room to soften that increase on year one.

I'd be open to hearing the numbers on that. I'll be honest with you though — and this is not meant as a threat, it's just where we are — we have been in some conversations with CyberNova over the last few weeks. Nothing formal, just exploratory. But my leadership is asking questions and I need to be able to make a strong case for staying.
I appreciate you being straight with us on that. Can I ask — is that conversation driven mainly by the outage, or is there something about the product itself that's prompting it?

Honestly, it's a mix. The outage was kind of a catalyst for the conversation internally — like, if we're questioning reliability, then we should be doing due diligence. But there are also some gaps I want to talk about with Comply. We're doing a CMMC audit later this year and I wasn't sure Comply was going to cover us on that.
Yeah, that's a good segue actually. So — Sofia, you want to touch on where Comply is heading? Because there's some relevant context there.

Sure. So we have Comply v2 coming out very shortly — the GA launch is April 7th. That release includes on-demand reporting for SOC 2, PCI DSS, HIPAA, and ISO 27001. The CMMC framework is not in that initial v2 launch, I want to be upfront about that. But it's on the roadmap and we're actively scoping it out. I don't want to give you a date I'm not confident in.
Okay, I appreciate the honesty. When you say it's on the roadmap — are we talking six months, twelve months? Because my audit is Q4.

My understanding is that CMMC is targeted for H2 this year but I would not want to commit you to a timeline without getting confirmation from our product team. What I'd suggest is that we get you a written roadmap commitment as part of the renewal discussion — and if the CMMC support doesn't land in time for your audit, we should talk about what that means for how we support you through that process manually or otherwise.
That's a reasonable position. I guess I'd want to see that in writing too. I've been burned before by roadmap promises that just... didn't land.

Understood. And that's — that's exactly the kind of thing I want to document. So let me just summarize what I'm hearing: enhanced SLA on Detect in writing, pricing structure that accounts for the outage context, and a written roadmap commitment on CMMC support tied to the renewal. Does that capture it?

Yeah, that's the bulk of it. And I guess just... look, the relationship matters. We've been on the platform for two and a half years. I'm not trying to blow that up over one bad month. But I do need the business case to be solid.

I hear you and I think that's completely reasonable. What I'll do is put together a revised proposal document by end of this week — so Thursday or Friday — that has the updated SLA language, the two-year pricing scenario, and whatever I can lock down in writing on the product roadmap side. You can share that with your leadership and your legal team.
That works. And if your product team can get on a call to walk through the CMMC roadmap directly, that might also be useful — either before or after I see the document.
I can set that up. I'll loop in our product manager on Comply and we can find a time in the next week or two.

Alright. That sounds like a plan. Let's get the document first and then we can figure out next steps from there. I don't want to drag this out — our current term ends April 30th so there's not a ton of runway.

Noted, and April 30th is very much on my radar. We'll get you something this week so you have time to review before it gets tight. Harold, is there anything else you want to flag before we let you go?
No, I think that covers the main stuff. Sofia, good to finally properly meet you. And Daniel — yeah, let's see what the proposal looks like.
Thanks Harold, same here. We'll be in touch.
Talk soon. Thanks for the time.
